headline: atomic task claims
sub: One agent claims a task and writes a lock. Nobody else touches it. That's how the collisions stop.

headline: the memory type nobody ships

cols.head: EPISODIC
cols.body: what happened. most agents have some of this.

cols.head: FRESHNESS
cols.body: is this doc still true? almost nobody tracks it.

source: MarkTechPost, The 7 Types of Agent Memory

headline: agents got better when i trusted them less
sub: Fewer ways to go wrong beat more ways to be clever. The constraint was the upgrade.
340,784 tokens saved last week across 91 doc-lookups. Routing instead of grepping, on our own fleet run.

headline: 9am: read doc. 11am: doc changed.
sub: Half the fleet is now acting on a version that's gone. How do you kill mid-task drift?

headline: the learning loop starts with killing stale context
sub: Re: VentureBeat, why agentic enterprises must become learning systems. You can't learn on top of rot.

headline: two agents, same config, 40 seconds apart
sub: No error. Silent disagreement. The fix: one doc to route through first.

headline: i trust an agent more when it has fewer ways to be wrong
sub: The trust came from the constraint, not the capability.

headline: a demo touches everything, a useful agent can't
sub: Re: the 'i connected my agent to my whole infra' threads on r/AI_Agents. Scope is the safety.

headline: ai-native is an org change, not a tool buy
sub: Readiness is one question: can your setup survive an agent acting on it?

headline: the real split is context, not headcount

cols.head: IN-HOUSE
cols.body: learns your system slowly, from the inside.

cols.head: OUTSIDE READ
cols.body: tells you in a week what's actually broken.

headline: what catches a wrong agent action first
sub: A review gate plus current context, not a bigger model. ~60% fewer tokens per lookup, one current answer.

headline: ai moved the cost, it didn't remove it
sub: Share of code rewritten or deleted within two weeks of being written.

headline: a readiness score has a shelf life
sub: Score, find the weak link, fix one, re-score. Loop it every time the ground moves: a new model, a new agent in the loop.
